reasoning: The community prediction as of late 2025 is 1%, strongly indicating that no 5-sigma violation has been found or is expected in the remaining two months before 2026. Internal knowledge confirms that the main piece of evidence against lepton universality, the LHCb anomaly (R_K, R_K*) at 3.1 sigma, was resolved and found to be consistent with the Standard Model in late 2022 after more data and better analysis algorithms. Given no current anomalies approaching the 5-sigma threshold and only two months left until the resolution date, it is virtually impossible for a peer-reviewed paper to be published claiming a 5-sigma violation of lepton universality.
